title: Chasing The Dream | 2026 Pre-Season Testing - Barcelona | F2 Behind The Scenes
description:

In this episode we caught up with IndyCar star Colton Herta, as he embarks on his first Formula 2 campaign, and some F2 favourites as the first laps of 2026 are completed in testing.

title: Carlos Sainz: Pushing For Progress With Williams | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
Five years after Carlos Sainz left McLaren, his former team and his former teammate are the reigning Formula 1 World Champions. As he watched Lando Norris reach the summit of world motorsport, did he ever think ‘that could have been me?’.

Speaking to Tom Clarkson, Carlos reflects on leaving McLaren and becoming a race winner with Ferrari before joining Williams. He remembers feeling ‘powerless’ during a difficult start to the 2025 season, and reveals the work which led him to stand on the podiu

title: 2026 F1 Engine & Battery Deployement Explained (MGU-K)
description:
F1 2026 just introduced one tiny word in the regulations — “pending” — and it completely changes how drivers use the throttle on corner exit.

In this video I break down what a power-limited pending period actually means in driver language, why the car no longer gives a sudden hybrid “kick”, and how the 150kW rule forces drivers to commit to throttle earlier instead of waiting for a boost moment.

You’ll learn:
• What “power-limited” vs “traction-limited” really feels like in the car
• Why the b

title: Max Verstappen Driving Incredibly for 70 Minutes
description:
Since joining F1 as a 17-year-old rookie, Max Verstappen has been destined for greatness. Already the holder of just about every record imaginable, enjoy an hour and 10 minutes of the Dutch master's very best racecraft in Formula 1... so far.

0:00 - Malaysia 2015, First Points
3:43 - Spain 2016, Red Bull debut win
9:00 - Brazil 2016, Epic comeback
16:45 - Austria 2018, Red Bull home win
22:08 - Austria 2019, Just an inchident
28:10 - Hungary 2022, Spin to win
32:25 - Belgium 2022, What penalty?

title: Every F1 Grand Slam of the 21st Century So Far
description:
A Grand Chelem - or Grand Slam - is the rarest achievement in Formula 1. Just 27 drivers have done it in the sport's history - and only 14 of those have achieved it more than once. Join us as we look back over the 24 times it's happened since the turn of the millennium in 2000!

0:00 - Michael Schumacher
4:26 - Fernando Alonso 
6:01 - Sebastian Vettel
12:14 - Lewis Hamilton
14:56 - Nico Rosberg
18:48 - Lewis Hamilton
25:13 - Max Verstappen
27:12 - Charles Leclerc
28:54 - Max Verstappen
